title: lolium arundinaceum subsp. orientale
intro: Lolium arundinaceum subsp. orientale, also known as Darnel or Oriental Ryegrass, is a subspecies of Lolium arundinaceum, a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Lolium arundinaceum subsp. orientale include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.
